articles.title: In a Reversal, Doctors From Countries Under Trump’s Travel Ban Are Allowed to Stay in U.S.
articles.summary: In a reversal, the Trump administration has exempted foreign physicians from a visa application freeze that was pushing many out of jobs in underserved areas.

articles.title: Taiwan's Lai lands in Eswatini in a trip delayed by lack of overflight clearance
articles.summary: Eswatini remains the only African nation without tariff-free access to China's market due to its ties with Taiwan.

articles.title: Indiana Primary Election Will Test Trump’s Influence With Republicans
articles.summary: The election on Tuesday will test the influence of President Trump, who endorsed challengers to Republican state senators who scuttled his push to redraw political maps.

articles.title: OPEC Plus, in Symbolic Gesture, to Increase Oil Production
articles.summary: The announcement came days after the United Arab Emirates withdrew from the group. The higher output will have little effect on global supplies.

articles.title: 'I just want to see her again' says son of imprisoned Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi
articles.summary: On Thursday, authorities in Myanmar claimed they had transferred Nobel laureate Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest. Her son Kim Aris spoke to NPR about his doubts about the regime's account.

articles.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
articles.summary: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money ba

articles.title: Trump Faces the Complicated Reality of a Costly, Unpopular War in Iran
articles.summary: President Trump’s predictions of a relatively short-term conflict with minimal economic consequences appear to be crumbling.

articles.title: Behind Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling, a Clash Over the Reality of Racism
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruling said there must be proof that a racial group was “intentionally” disadvantaged. The dissent called it “well-nigh impossible.”

articles.title: A California Dream? Some Democrats Fear Harris Picked the Wrong Race.
articles.summary: Some wish Kamala Harris had decided to run for governor in California, where Democrats are struggling to break through, rather than weigh another White House run.

articles.title: MAHA Awaits Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Definition of Ultraprocessed Foods
articles.summary: Some yogurts and peanut butters could be considered ultraprocessed as scientists and MAHA supporters push for a strict definition.

articles.title: Why this tribe is buying up hundreds of acres of farmland — and flooding it
articles.summary: The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon

articles.title: How algorithms wreaked havoc with these workers' schedules and cut their pay
articles.summary: Hourly workers across a number of industries have long been grappling with unstable schedules and pay as their employers use software to slash labor costs and maximize productivity.

articles.title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
articles.summary: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can

articles.title: Bard College's president to retire after scrutiny of relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
articles.summary: The longtime president of Bard College in New York has announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship than was previously known with Jeffrey Epstein.

articles.title: Golden Tempo takes the Kentucky Derby as Cherie DeVaux becomes the 1st woman to train its winner
articles.summary: Golden Tempo has won the Kentucky Derby at odds of 23-1 to make Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

articles.title: Iran submits 14-point response to U.S. proposal to end war
articles.summary: Iran has presented a 14-point response to the U.S. proposal to end the war, according to Iranian state media.

articles.title: Is there a 'standard' Indian body?
articles.summary: India recently published a survey to 'standardize' the Indian body sizes, so its manufacturers don't have to use the U.S. and E.U. sizes for a billion people. But is there a 'standard' Indian body?

articles.title: Collaboration helps NPR journalists get you the story of the Iran war up close
articles.summary: Three NPR journalists talk about the challenge of producing independent, accurate coverage of the war with Iran.

articles.title: ICRC President Mirjana Spoljaric warns of the war's impact on civilians
articles.summary: The ICRC president talks about her recent trip to Iran, and warns about the impact of the Iran war on civilians.

articles.title: Banksy confirms new statue installed in central London is his work
articles.summary: The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.

articles.title: Jordanians Struggle as Mideast Wars Scare Tourists Away
articles.summary: Though Jordan mostly sat out the conflicts, its tourism high season was nearly wiped out at popular sites. Visitors canceled flights, hotels and tours.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
